name: grid-container
role: Auto-fit grid container. Cells reflow into rows + columns based on container width and per-cell minimum width; no media queries required. The foundation for feature-grid, pricing-tier-grid, card lists, image galleries, dashboard widget grids.

min-cell.note: narrow=200px, standard=280px (default — fits 1–3 columns at common widths), wide=360px. 'custom' lets the operator set --prim-grid-min-cell directly on the element or an ancestor.

max-columns.note: Caps the maximum column count. 'unlimited' = no cap (true auto-fit, fills as many columns as fit). When capped, the grid uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(min(max-cell-width, 100%), 1fr)) — preserves the auto-fit behavior up to the cap.

align.note: Cross-axis (vertical) alignment of grid items. 'stretch' (default) makes all cells in a row equal height.

semanticStructure: <!-- grid cells; cells are typically articles or list items -->
meta.cssRecipe: display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(min(var(--prim-grid-min-cell, 280px), 100%), 1fr)); gap: var(--prim-grid-gap, var(--space-card));
meta.minMaxRationale: The inner min(min-cell, 100%) prevents overflow on viewports narrower than min-cell — without it, a 200px min-cell on a 150px viewport would cause horizontal scroll.

ariaPatterns.noRole: grid-container is structural. It is NOT a [role='grid'] (which has different semantic + interaction contracts — data-table's grid role). For data-grid behavior, use pattern-data-table.
ariaPatterns.semanticOuter: Operators wrap the grid in <ul> when cells are list items, or leave it as <div> when cells are independent articles.

meta.deprecationOpportunity: feature-grid pattern currently inlines this auto-fit grid recipe. Future consolidation (deferred, call it Phase 22.7) refactors feature-grid to compose prim-grid. Not in this session's scope.
